metadata.name: CDE Workflow
metadata.description: Information container states and transitions within the Common Data Environment as defined in ISO 19650-1 Clause 12.
metadata.author: ISO19650 Knowledge Maps

nodes.label: Information Container
nodes.description: Named persistent set of information retrievable from within a file, system or application storage hierarchy.

nodes.label: Work In Progress
nodes.description: State where information is being developed by its originating task team and is not visible or accessible to other teams.

nodes.label: Shared
nodes.description: State where information is shared with other teams for coordination and collaborative development.

nodes.label: Published
nodes.description: State where information has been authorized for use in subsequent stages, construction, or asset operation.

nodes.label: Archived
nodes.description: State providing a journal of all information container transactions and an audit trail of their development.

nodes.label: Common Data Environment
nodes.description: Agreed source of information for collecting, managing and disseminating each information container through a managed process.

nodes.label: Task Team
nodes.description: Individuals assembled to perform a specific task, responsible for checking/reviewing their own information.

evidence.quote: Information containers are developed within the work in progress state of the common data environment.
The work in progress state is used for information while it is being developed by its task team.

The check/review/approve transition compares the information container against the information delivery plan and against the agreed standards, methods and procedures for producing information. The check/review/approve transition should be made by the originating task team.
Information is shared for coordination once it has been checked, reviewed and approved for sharing.

The review/authorize transition compares all the information containers at information exchange against the relevant information requirements for coordination, completeness and accuracy. If an information container meets the information requirements, its state is changed to published.
The archive state is used to hold a journal of all information containers that have been shared and published during the information management process as well as an audit trail of their development.
The common data environment is the agreed source of information for the project or asset.
The common data environment manages information through defined states.
Authorized information is made available through the common data environment.
The archive state provides a journal of all information container transactions and an audit trail of their development.
If editing is required, an information container should be returned to the work in progress state for amendment and resubmission by its author.

An information container referenced in the archive state that was previously in the published state represents information that potentially has been used for more detailed design work, for construction or for asset management.
